news.title: Constellation Energy Deepens Data Center Ties With Calpine Deal And Nuclear Upgrades
news.summary: Constellation Energy (NasdaqGS:CEG) completed a US$26.6b acquisition of Calpine, expanding its generation footprint and customer reach. The company signed a long term 380 MW power supply agreement with data center developer CyrusOne targeting large scale facilities. Regulators approved upgrades at key nuclear facilities, clearing the way for higher capacity and reliability from carbon free assets. For you as an investor, the appeal of Constellation Energy often starts with its role as a...

news.title: Constellation Energy Surges 10% This Week on Data Center Deals and Analyst Upgrades
news.summary: Constellation Energy Corp (NASDAQ:CEG) surged 10.33% this week, closing at $288.43 on Friday, February 13. The move reversed recent losses, though the stock remains down 18.35% year-to-date. Three storylines drove the week: major data center power deals, bullish analyst calls, and renewed conviction around nuclear energy’s role in powering AI infrastructure. news.title: Constellation Energy Texas Data Center Deal Tests Valuation And Concentration Risks
news.summary: Constellation Energy’s Calpine unit has secured a new power agreement with data center operator CyrusOne in Texas. The deal covers up to 760 megawatts of power capacity for CyrusOne’s planned Texas data centers. The agreement includes exclusive rights for potential further expansion tied to CyrusOne’s future facilities in the state. For Constellation Energy, ticker NasdaqGS:CEG, this new agreement arrives after a mixed run for the share price. The stock closed at $276.12, with an 11.8% gain...

news.title: Constellation to Post Q4 Earnings: What's in Store for the Stock?
news.summary: CEG nears Q4 results with revenues up 1.9% and a +3.13% Earnings ESP. The company enjoys the benefit of strong demand from data centers.
